appraisal_events.evidence: Le salut de Robert est « précipité » et « brusque »; l’officier est « calme, bienveillant, digne, impérial, représentant en somme tout l’opposé de Robert de Saint-Loup ».
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator contrasts Saint-Loup’s nervousness and jerky gestures with the poised and majestic bearing of the officer, which locally presents Saint-Loup as lacking the dignity expected in this context.

status_effects.explanation: By contrast with the officer described as the « opposé » of him, Saint-Loup appears less controlled and less dignified, which slightly lowers his local estimation.
